specification.topic-statement: The agent database layer persists agent lifecycle status transitions, provider configuration, and idle state to PostgreSQL, and retrieves agent records for session restoration and navigation.

scope.in-scope: Agent record insertion, status transitions (running → dead → reaped), idle flag management, provider and model update, and retrieval queries (single agent, session-scoped list, global list).
scope.boundary: When state transitions occur in memory (IDLE, WAITING_FOR_LLM, EXECUTING_TOOL) is managed by the agent state machine. This topic covers only the persistent database representation.
The agents and messages tables are created by the migration system. This topic assumes the schema exists.

field.name: ended at
field.note: Unix timestamp when marked dead; zero when not yet dead

field.name: fork message id
field.note: identifier of the message in the parent's history at which this agent was forked; zero for the root agent

field.name: thinking level
field.note: one of: low, med, high, min

behavior.agent-insertion: A new agent record is inserted with status "running", the provided identifier, name, parent identifier, session identifier, fork message id, and creation timestamp. The thinking level integer is mapped to a string at insertion time: 1 maps to "low", 2 to "med", 3 to "high", and any other value maps to "min".

status-transitions.mark-dead: The agent's status is changed to "dead" and an ended-at timestamp is recorded. The update targets only agents whose current status is "running". If the agent is already dead (or reaped), the update matches no rows and the operation succeeds silently without error. This makes the operation idempotent.
status-transitions.mark-reaped:
A single named agent's status is changed to "reaped". The update is unconditional: it does not check the agent's current status. Any status value (including "running") is overwritten.
Unlike mark-dead, mark-reaped has no precondition on the current status. An agent can be transitioned to "reaped" from any state.
status-transitions.reap-all-dead: All agents with status "dead" are updated to status "reaped" in a single bulk operation. No agent identifier is specified; every dead agent in the database is affected.

behavior.idle-flag: The idle flag is a boolean on each agent record that is independent of the status field. It can be set to either true or false. There is no precondition on the current idle value or on the agent's status. The flag is updated unconditionally by identifier.
behavior.provider-update:
The provider name, model name, and thinking level for an agent can be updated atomically. Any of the three fields may be null. If the agent identifier does not match any record, the update affects no rows and returns success without error.
The caller cannot distinguish between a successful update and a no-op caused by the agent not existing.

retrieval.get-single-agent: A single agent record is retrieved by identifier. All fields are returned. If no matching record exists, an error is returned. Null ended-at timestamps in the database are coerced to zero in the returned record.
retrieval.list-running-agents: All agents with status "running" are returned, ordered by creation timestamp. This query is not scoped to any session; it returns running agents from all sessions in the database. If no running agents exist, an empty result is returned without error.
retrieval.list-active-agents:
Agents with status "running" or "dead" belonging to a specific session are returned, ordered by creation timestamp. Agents with status "reaped" are excluded. This query is session-scoped.
List-running returns agents globally across all sessions, while list-active is confined to a single session. The distinction determines which operation is appropriate for restoration versus session-local display.
retrieval.get-last-message-id: The highest message identifier associated with a given agent is returned by querying the messages table rather than the agents table. If the agent has no messages, zero is returned. This value is used to record the fork point when creating a child agent.
